      23              : 
      24              : 
      25              : using namespace bssl;
      26              : 
      27              : // The 32-bit hash algorithms share a common byte-order neutral collector and
      28              : // padding function implementations that operate on unaligned data,
      29              : // ../digest/md32_common.h. SHA-512 is the only 64-bit hash algorithm, as of
      30              : // this writing, so there is no need for a common collector/padding
      31              : // implementation yet.
      32              : 
      33              : static void sha512_final_impl(uint8_t *out, size_t md_len, SHA512_CTX *sha);
      34              :

     127              : bcm_infallible bssl::BCM_sha512_update(SHA512_CTX *c, const void *in_data,
     128            0 :                                        size_t len) {
     129            0 :   uint8_t *p = c->p;
     130            0 :   const uint8_t *data = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(in_data);
     131              : 
     132            0 :   if (len == 0) {
     133            0 :     return bcm_infallible::approved;
     134            0 :   }
     135              : 
     136            0 :   c->bytes_so_far_low += len;
     137            0 :   if (c->bytes_so_far_low < len) {
     138            0 :     c->bytes_so_far_high++;
     139            0 :   }
     140              : 
     141            0 :   if (c->num != 0) {
     142            0 :     size_t n = sizeof(c->p) - c->num;
     143              : 
     144            0 :     if (len < n) {
     145            0 :       OPENSSL_memcpy(p + c->num, data, len);
     146            0 :       c->num += (unsigned int)len;
     147            0 :       return bcm_infallible::approved;
     148            0 :     } else {
     149            0 :       OPENSSL_memcpy(p + c->num, data, n), c->num = 0;
     150            0 :       len -= n;
     151            0 :       data += n;
     152            0 :       sha512_block_data_order(c->h, p, 1);
     153            0 :     }
     154            0 :   }
     155              : 
     156            0 :   if (len >= sizeof(c->p)) {
     157            0 :     sha512_block_data_order(c->h, data, len / sizeof(c->p));

     159            0 :     len %= sizeof(c->p);
     160            0 :     data -= len;
     161            0 :   }
     162              : 
     163            0 :   if (len != 0) {
     164            0 :     OPENSSL_memcpy(p, data, len);
     165            0 :     c->num = (int)len;
     166            0 :   }
     167              : 
     168            0 :   return bcm_infallible::approved;
     169            0 : }
     170              : 
     171              : bcm_infallible bssl::BCM_sha512_final(uint8_t out[SHA512_DIGEST_LENGTH],
     172            0 :                                       SHA512_CTX *sha) {
     173              :   // Ideally we would assert |sha->md_len| is |SHA512_DIGEST_LENGTH| to match
     174              :   // the size hint, but calling code often pairs |BCM_sha384_init| with
     175              :   // |BCM_sha512_final| and expects |sha->md_len| to carry the size over.
     176              :   //
     177              :   // TODO(davidben): Add an assert and fix code to match them up.
     178            0 :   sha512_final_impl(out, sha->md_len, sha);
     179            0 :   return bcm_infallible::approved;
     180            0 : }
     181              : 
     182            0 : static void sha512_final_impl(uint8_t *out, size_t md_len, SHA512_CTX *sha) {
     183            0 :   uint8_t *p = sha->p;
     184            0 :   size_t n = sha->num;
     185              : 
     186            0 :   p[n] = 0x80;  // There always is a room for one
     187            0 :   n++;
     188            0 :   if (n > (sizeof(sha->p) - 16)) {
     189            0 :     OPENSSL_memset(p + n, 0, sizeof(sha->p) - n);
     190            0 :     n = 0;
     191            0 :     sha512_block_data_order(sha->h, p, 1);
     192            0 :   }
     193              : 
     194            0 :   OPENSSL_memset(p + n, 0, sizeof(sha->p) - 16 - n);
     195            0 :   const uint64_t Nh = (uint64_t{sha->bytes_so_far_high} << 3) |
     196            0 :                       (sha->bytes_so_far_low >> (64 - 3));
     197            0 :   const uint64_t Nl = sha->bytes_so_far_low << 3;
     198            0 :   CRYPTO_store_u64_be(p + sizeof(sha->p) - 16, Nh);
     199            0 :   CRYPTO_store_u64_be(p + sizeof(sha->p) - 8, Nl);
     200              : 
     201            0 :   sha512_block_data_order(sha->h, p, 1);
     202              : 
     203            0 :   assert(md_len % 8 == 0);
     204            0 :   const size_t out_words = md_len / 8;
     205            0 :   for (size_t i = 0; i < out_words; i++) {
     206            0 :     CRYPTO_store_u64_be(out, sha->h[i]);
     207            0 :     out += 8;
     208            0 :   }
     209              : 
     210            0 :   FIPS_service_indicator_update_state();
     211            0 : }

     258              : 
     259              : #define Sigma0(x)                                        \
     260            0 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 28) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 34) ^ \
     261            0 :    CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 39))
     262              : #define Sigma1(x)                                        \
     263            0 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 14) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 18) ^ \
     264            0 :    CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 41))
     265              : #define sigma0(x) \
     266            0 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 1) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 8) ^ ((x) >> 7))
     267              : #define sigma1(x) \
     268            0 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 19) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u64((x), 61) ^ ((x) >> 6))
     269              : 
     270            0 : #define Ch(x, y, z) (((x) & (y)) ^ ((~(x)) & (z)))
     271            0 : #define Maj(x, y, z) (((x) & (y)) ^ ((x) & (z)) ^ ((y) & (z)))
     272              : 
     273              : 
     274              : #if defined(__i386) || defined(__i386__) || defined(_M_IX86)
     275              : // This code should give better results on 32-bit CPU with less than
     276              : // ~24 registers, both size and performance wise...
     277              : static void sha512_block_data_order_nohw(uint64_t state[8], const uint8_t *in,
     278              :                                          size_t num) {
     279              :   uint64_t A, E, T;
     280              :   uint64_t X[9 + 80], *F;
     281              :   int i;
     282              : 
     283              :   while (num--) {
     284              :     F = X + 80;
     285              :     A = state[0];
     286              :     F[1] = state[1];
     287              :     F[2] = state[2];
     288              :     F[3] = state[3];
     289              :     E = state[4];
     290              :     F[5] = state[5];
     291              :     F[6] = state[6];
     292              :     F[7] = state[7];
     293              : 
     294              :     for (i = 0; i < 16; i++, F--) {
     295              :       T = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + i * 8);
     296              :       F[0] = A;
     297              :       F[4] = E;
     298              :       F[8] = T;
     299              :       T += F[7] + Sigma1(E) + Ch(E, F[5], F[6]) + K512[i];
     300              :       E = F[3] + T;
     301              :       A = T + Sigma0(A) + Maj(A, F[1], F[2]);
     302              :     }
     303              : 
     304              :     for (; i < 80; i++, F--) {
     305              :       T = sigma0(F[8 + 16 - 1]);
     306              :       T += sigma1(F[8 + 16 - 14]);
     307              :       T += F[8 + 16] + F[8 + 16 - 9];
     308              : 
     309              :       F[0] = A;
     310              :       F[4] = E;
     311              :       F[8] = T;
     312              :       T += F[7] + Sigma1(E) + Ch(E, F[5], F[6]) + K512[i];
     313              :       E = F[3] + T;
     314              :       A = T + Sigma0(A) + Maj(A, F[1], F[2]);
     315              :     }
     316              : 
     317              :     state[0] += A;
     318              :     state[1] += F[1];
     319              :     state[2] += F[2];
     320              :     state[3] += F[3];
     321              :     state[4] += E;
     322              :     state[5] += F[5];
     323              :     state[6] += F[6];
     324              :     state[7] += F[7];
     325              : 
     326              :     in += 16 * 8;
     327              :   }
     328              : }
     329              : 
     330              : #else
     331              : 
     332              : #define ROUND_00_15(i, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h)   \
     333            0 :   do {                                           \
     334            0 :     T1 += h + Sigma1(e) + Ch(e, f, g) + K512[i]; \
     335            0 :     h = Sigma0(a) + Maj(a, b, c);                \
     336            0 :     d += T1;                                     \
     337            0 :     h += T1;                                     \
     338            0 :   } while (0)
     339              : 
     340              : #define ROUND_16_80(i, j, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, X)   \
     341            0 :   do {                                                 \
     342            0 :     s0 = X[(j + 1) & 0x0f];                            \
     343            0 :     s0 = sigma0(s0);                                   \
     344            0 :     s1 = X[(j + 14) & 0x0f];                           \
     345            0 :     s1 = sigma1(s1);                                   \
     346            0 :     T1 = X[(j) & 0x0f] += s0 + s1 + X[(j + 9) & 0x0f]; \
     347            0 :     ROUND_00_15(i + j, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h);        \
     348            0 :   } while (0)
     349              : 
     350              : static void sha512_block_data_order_nohw(uint64_t state[8], const uint8_t *in,
     351            0 :                                          size_t num) {
     352            0 :   uint64_t a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, s0, s1, T1;
     353            0 :   uint64_t X[16];
     354            0 :   int i;
     355              : 
     356            0 :   while (num--) {
     357            0 :     a = state[0];
     358            0 :     b = state[1];
     359            0 :     c = state[2];
     360            0 :     d = state[3];
     361            0 :     e = state[4];
     362            0 :     f = state[5];
     363            0 :     g = state[6];
     364            0 :     h = state[7];
     365              : 
     366            0 :     T1 = X[0] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in);
     367            0 :     ROUND_00_15(0, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h);
     368            0 :     T1 = X[1] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 8);
     369            0 :     ROUND_00_15(1, h, a, b, c, d, e, f, g);
     370            0 :     T1 = X[2] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 2 * 8);
     371            0 :     ROUND_00_15(2, g, h, a, b, c, d, e, f);
     372            0 :     T1 = X[3] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 3 * 8);
     373            0 :     ROUND_00_15(3, f, g, h, a, b, c, d, e);
     374            0 :     T1 = X[4] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 4 * 8);
     375            0 :     ROUND_00_15(4, e, f, g, h, a, b, c, d);
     376            0 :     T1 = X[5] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 5 * 8);
     377            0 :     ROUND_00_15(5, d, e, f, g, h, a, b, c);
     378            0 :     T1 = X[6] = CRYPTO_load_u64_be(in + 6 * 8);

     411            0 :       ROUND_16_80(i, 11, f, g, h, a, b, c, d, e, X);
     412            0 :       ROUND_16_80(i, 12, e, f, g, h, a, b, c, d, X);
     413            0 :       ROUND_16_80(i, 13, d, e, f, g, h, a, b, c, X);
     414            0 :       ROUND_16_80(i, 14, c, d, e, f, g, h, a, b, X);
     415            0 :       ROUND_16_80(i, 15, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, a, X);
     416            0 :     }
     417              : 
     418            0 :     state[0] += a;
     419            0 :     state[1] += b;
     420            0 :     state[2] += c;
     421            0 :     state[3] += d;

     423            0 :     state[5] += f;

     425            0 :     state[7] += h;

     429            0 : }
     430              : 
     431              : #endif
     432              : 
     433              : #endif  // !SHA512_ASM_NOHW
     434              : 
     435              : static void sha512_block_data_order(uint64_t state[8], const uint8_t *data,
     436            0 :                                     size_t num) {
     437              : #if defined(SHA512_ASM_HW)
     438              :   if (sha512_hw_capable()) {
     439              :     sha512_block_data_order_hw(state, data, num);
     440              :     return;
     441              :   }
     442              : #endif
     443              : #if defined(SHA512_ASM_AVX)
     444              :   if (sha512_avx_capable()) {
     445              :     sha512_block_data_order_avx(state, data, num);
     446              :     return;
     447              :   }
     448              : #endif
     449              : #if defined(SHA512_ASM_SSSE3)
     450              :   if (sha512_ssse3_capable()) {
     451              :     sha512_block_data_order_ssse3(state, data, num);
     452              :     return;
     453              :   }
     454              : #endif
     455              : #if defined(SHA512_ASM_NEON)
     456              :   if (CRYPTO_is_NEON_capable()) {
     457              :     sha512_block_data_order_neon(state, data, num);
     458              :     return;
     459              :   }
     460              : #endif
     461            0 :   sha512_block_data_order_nohw(state, data, num);
     462            0 : }
     463              : 
     464              : #endif  // !SHA512_ASM
     465              :
